summary: Large mpcoll matching PR centered on src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c, with 42 new matches and one near-match improvement reported by decomp.dev. The work added or completed many collision routines, corrected mpLib/mpColl prototypes, updated affected fighter/item/stage callers, and used inline/dont_inline and stack-shape tactics to make a long block of map collision code match.
what_changed: Implemented or matched a large set of mpcoll functions in src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c, replacing many placeholder declarations with concrete code for routines around mpColl_80043F40, mpColl_80044164, fn_80046F78, mpColl_800488F4, mpColl_8004AB80, fn_8004ACE4, mpColl_8004B6D8, mpColl_8004C750, and mpColl_8004D024.
decomp.dev reported matched code increased to 42.40%, +0.30% and +11716 bytes, with 42 new matches in main/melee/mp/mpcoll and mpColl_80044164 improving from 0.00% to 96.05%.

Updated mpcoll.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or incorrect scalar return types to typed bool/s32/float signatures for many collision functions, including fn_80046F78(CollData*, u32), fn_8004ACE4(CollData*, int), mpColl_800488F4(CollData*), mpColl_8004B6D8(CollData*), and mpColl_8004D024(Vec3*).

Corrected static globals in mpcoll.c: mpColl_804D64A0 became a bool callback pointer taking Fighter_GObj* and int, mpColl_804D64A4 became Fighter_GObj*, mpColl_804D64AC became int, and NULL/true/false were used instead of loosely typed 0/1 values.

Updated mplib.h signatures so map-collision helpers accept Vec3* and typed coordinate/flag arguments instead of float* or UNK_PARAMS, notably mpLib_8004DD90, mpLib_8004E398, mpLib_8004E684, mpLib_8004F008, mpLib_8004F400, mpLib_80051BA8, mpLib_80052508, mpLib_80054584, mpLib_8005484C, and mpLib_800569EC.
Adjusted callers to match corrected mpLib_8004F008 argument order: ftCo_800A0FB0 and mpIsland_8005AC14 now pass the float segment coordinates before the index/filter arguments.
Adjusted item collision caller it_802759DC to pass &sp44 as a Vec3* to mpLib_8004DD90 instead of &sp44.x as a float pointer.

Reworked parts of mpcoll.c to use Collide_* flag names such as Collide_Edge, Collide_LeftEdge, Collide_RightEdge, Collide_CeilingHug, Collide_CeilingPush, Collide_FloorPush, Collide_LeftWallPush, and Collide_RightWallPush instead of raw env_flags masks where visible in the diff.
Removed the mpColl_804D3948 assert string global and used literal "0" in __assert calls.
smart_moves: Introduced mpColl_80042D24_inline(CollData* coll, enum_t i) to share the ECB update pattern while allowing callers to vary the mpColl_800424DC argument; retained the public mpColl_80042D24 with #pragma dont_inline on, preserving matching-sensitive call structure.

Converted large clusters of thin public collision entry points into small wrappers around shared inline helpers, reducing repeated logic while still matching compiler output for variants such as mpColl_800471F8, mpColl_800473CC, mpColl_80047AC8, mpColl_80047BF4, and related functions.
Fixed header prototypes first-class enough that downstream callsites could be made type-correct, which exposed and corrected argument-order mistakes in ftCo_800A0FB0 and mpIsland_8005AC14.
Used typed Vec3 locals where earlier code split coordinates into separate float temporaries, enabling correct calls to mpLib_8004E398, mpLib_8004DD90, mpLib_800501CC, and related geometry helpers.
Replaced ambiguous Event/void casts for callback storage with explicit bool callback signatures and Fighter_GObj* storage, improving both matching clarity and future callsite safety.
Used named collision flags in conditionals and assignments, making the matched code more maintainable without relying only on raw bitmasks.
decomp_lessons: For mpcoll work, correct mplib geometry prototypes are high leverage: one Vec3* vs float* or reordered coordinate/filter signature can affect multiple systems and prevent clean matches.

When a repeated collision wrapper pattern differs only by a small mode/index argument, a static inline helper plus a dont_inline public function can reproduce compiler behavior while keeping source readable.
Do not leave callback globals typed as generic Event or cast-through void unless evidence requires it; typed function pointers can unlock matches and document the actual calling convention.
For ECB/collision code, prefer semantically named CollData* coll and flag names over arg0 and numeric masks once the structure and enum meanings are known.
A local Vec3 can be necessary for stack layout and prototype correctness when decompiling geometry routines that consume both x/y coordinates and normals.
Literal assert strings can be better than keeping artificial string globals if the global was only a matching crutch and the literal matches the target data layout.
naming_conventions: Use CollData* coll for collision data parameters where meaning is known, replacing generic arg0/cdata in newly matched routines.
Use p_ledge_id for output pointer parameters in ledge-check helpers such as mpColl_80044164 and mpColl_800443C4.
Use bool return types for collision query/result functions when the matched behavior is predicate-like, replacing UNK_RET or s32 where evidence supports it.
Use Vec3* in mplib prototypes for position/point arguments instead of float* when callers pass whole vector locals.
Use typed callback signatures like bool (*)(Fighter_GObj*, int) or bool (*)(Fighter_GObj*, enum_t) for fighter collision callbacks.

Prefer named collision flag constants Collide_Edge, Collide_LeftEdge, Collide_RightEdge, Collide_CeilingHug, Collide_CeilingPush, Collide_FloorPush, Collide_LeftWallPush, and Collide_RightWallPush over raw hex masks.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Applied #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around mpColl_80042D24 to keep a public function from being inlined while still using inline equivalents elsewhere.

Added static inline helper variants, including mpColl_80042D24_inline, mpColl_80044164_inline, fn_80046F78_inline, and several inline0/inline1/inline2/inline3/inline4 wrappers, to control duplicated codegen patterns.
Kept a documented dummy-stack issue around func_80043324_inline2 and mpColl_80043558, indicating stack layout was actively managed for matching.
Changed dummy_string_data from const char* to char[] for the "i<MPCOLL_WALLID_MAX" string, a data-layout tactic that can affect rodata/data placement.
Reordered locals in geometry routines such as mpColl_80043C6C from separate float slots to a Vec3 plus scalar temporaries, aligning stack use with corrected mplib signatures.
Simplified boolean comparisons from != 0 to direct truth tests in several spots, consistent with matching compiler output after types were corrected.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

Automated decomp.dev feedback reported 42 new matches and +11716 matched bytes, with notable new matches including fn_8004ACE4, fn_80046F78, mpColl_80043F40, mpColl_800488F4, mpColl_8004B6D8, mpColl_8004AB80, mpColl_8004D024, and many wrapper-style mpColl_80047xxx/mpColl_80048xxx functions.
The automated report also noted mpColl_80044164 improved to 96.05% but was not fully matched in this PR.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish mpColl_80044164 from 96.05% to 100%.
Search for subsequent mplib.h prototype corrections around mpLib_8004F400, mpLib_800542BC, mpLib_80054420, and mpLib_8005484C.
Inspect callers of mpLib_8004F008 for remaining argument-order mismatches after this PR.
Look for later renames of fn_80046F78, fn_8004ACE4, and fn_8004C534 once behavior is better understood.
Search for other mpcoll wrapper matches that reuse mpColl_80042D24_inline or the inline0/inline1/inline2/inline3/inline4 pattern.
